title: [Vaporeon] Battle Guide
summary: A massive-HP [Water] [wall] that locks down [Water] attackers via [water-absorb] and runs a full [wish] and [protect] [recover]y loop for the team.

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Vaporeon] is a [Water]-type [wall] built around its massive 130 base HP and the [water-absorb] ability, which makes it a natural answer to any team leaning on [Water] STAB offense. It isn't an offensive Pokemon in the traditional sense — its role is team healing and board control through the [wish] and [protect] loop, keeping partner Pokemon alive over a long game. 60 raw Defense looks thin, but combined with the HP stat the effective physical bulk is much higher than it looks.

## Strengths
[water-absorb] doesn't just null the damage from [Water] moves — it restores 25 percent HP on contact, which means opposing [Water] attackers actively stabilize [Vaporeon] instead of threatening it. The [wish] move is the main [support] tool in the tier: it heals an incoming ally for half of [Vaporeon]'s max HP on the next turn, which keeps sweepers like setup attackers online through a long game. [protect] covers the [wish] turn and stalls passive damage like [toxic] ticks or weather chip.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Electric] and [Grass] hit for 2x, and any special attacker running [thunderbolt] or [energy-ball] is a real threat. The raw Defense stat is 60, so a strong physical breaker is dangerous if it bypasses the HP pool. Offensively [Vaporeon] barely pressures any [wall], which means [taunt] users can neuter it. Running out of [wish] PP in a long game is also an edge case worth watching.

## Recommended Moveset
[scald] is the locked [Water] STAB because its burn chance makes physical attackers think twice about pressing the attack button. [wish] is the core [support] slot. [protect] covers [wish] turns and stalls, and [ice-beam] fills the last slot with [Grass], [Dragon], and [Ground] coverage. [heal-bell] is an alternate last slot if the team desperately needs status removal over coverage.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default. [sitrus-berry] is an alternate for one big healing pulse when HP drops below 50 percent, useful when the team needs [Vaporeon] alive at half rather than healing forever. The ability must be [water-absorb]. Tera [Fairy] gives resistances to [Fighting] and [Dragon], while Tera [Ground] flips [Electric] weakness into immunity.

## Teammates & Synergy
Wish [support] works best on defensive cores, so a [Steel] [wall] that absorbs physical hits for [Vaporeon] is the natural partner. An [Electric] answer. Cleaners on the back line convert the [wish] sustain into wins, and a teammate with [heal-bell] reduces status pressure if you didn't run it on [Vaporeon] itself.

## Play Patterns
The basic cycle is a two-turn loop: [wish] turn one, [protect] turn two, and pass the heal to a partner by switching out on turn three. Let opposing [Water] attackers in freely to trigger [water-absorb], and switch out when an [Electric] or [Grass] attacker is about to come in. Save the Tera for the exact moment a [thunderbolt] or [energy-ball] is about to land.
